description: Fix inaccurate documentation: wrong code examples, incorrect file locations, outdated build pipeline descriptions, and stale publish script steps
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix this.version reference in lifecycle-hooks.mdx code example
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the lifecycle hooks full example to compile so that I can copy it as a starting point
acceptanceCriteria: The onActivate() code example in lifecycle-hooks.mdx no longer references this.version
The replacement uses a property that exists on OpenTabsPlugin (e.g., this.name) or a plain string literal
The overall example still demonstrates assigning a global handle in onActivate and cleaning it up in teardown
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx, line 288. The code example has `(window as Record<string, unknown>).__analyticsAdapter = { version: this.version };` but OpenTabsPlugin (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts) has no `version` property. Replace `this.version` with `this.name` (which exists as an abstract readonly property on OpenTabsPlugin). This is a simple text substitution in the MDX file.

userStories.title: Fix OpenCode config file location in quick-start.mdx
userStories.description: As a user setting up OpenCode, I want the correct config file path so that I can configure my MCP client
acceptanceCriteria: The MCP client config table in quick-start.mdx lists OpenCode's config file as 'opencode.json in project root' (not ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json)
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx, line 53. The table row for OpenCode says `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` but the actual source (platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts, line 189) generates the config with `file: 'opencode.json in project root'`. Change the table cell to `opencode.json` with a note that it goes in the project root, matching what `opentabs start` actually outputs.

userStories.title: Fix CustomEvent target in streaming-progress.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the progress flow documentation to accurately describe the event target so that I can debug progress reporting issues
acceptanceCriteria: Step 2 of the progress flow in streaming-progress.mdx says 'fires a CustomEvent on document' (not 'on window')
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx, line 124. The text says 'Adapter IIFE fires a CustomEvent on window' but the actual source (platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.ts, line 226) does `document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent(...))`. Change 'window' to 'document' in that sentence. The parenthetical '(MAIN world → ISOLATED world)' can stay — CustomEvents on document are still visible cross-world.

userStories.title: Add missing browser-tools-catalog step to dev-setup.mdx build pipeline
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the build pipeline documentation to list all steps so that I understand what npm run build actually does
acceptanceCriteria: The build pipeline list in dev-setup.mdx includes a step for 'Browser tools catalog generation' (npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog) between tsc --build and the extension bundle step
The list now shows 7 steps matching the actual build script in root package.json
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx, lines 31-38. The numbered list shows 6 steps but the actual build script (root package.json line 19) has 7 steps. The missing step is `npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog` which runs between step 1 (tsc --build) and step 2 (extension bundle). Insert a new step 2: 'Browser tools catalog — generates the static browser tool catalog used by the side panel' and renumber the remaining steps 3-7.

userStories.title: Fix publish script steps in publishing.mdx
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the publishing documentation to accurately describe what the publish script does so that I understand the release process
acceptanceCriteria: The publish script steps list in publishing.mdx matches the actual script at scripts/publish.ts

Step order is: (1) verify npm auth, (2) bump versions, (3) sync lockfile and rebuild with build:force, (4) publish in dependency order, (5) update plugin dependencies and rebuild, (6) generate changelog, (7) create release commit and tag
The text says 'npm run build:force' (not 'npm run build')
Steps 5-7 (plugin updates, changelog, git commit/tag) are included
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx, lines 90-97. The current list describes 6 steps that don't match the actual script (scripts/publish.ts). The actual script (verified at lines 239-380) does: (1) verify npm auth via npm whoami (line 240), (2) bump versions in all 7 platform/*/package.json files (line 255), (3) delete lockfile + npm install + npm run build:force (lines 263-272, NOT 'npm run build'), (4) publish in dependency order (line 277), (5) update plugin deps to ^version and rebuild plugins (line 289), (6) generate changelog from git log (line 324), (7) create git commit and tag (line 360). Replace the current 6-step list with these 7 steps. The current doc incorrectly says 'Runs npm run build' twice and misses steps 5-7 entirely.
